################################################################################
# batch_ak.txt — vehicle + ai C1->C2 drain (next two closeable subsystems)
#
# Generated by the discover-c1-batch skill, 2026-06-02.
# Work type:    promote-c2 (C1 -> C2 mechanical promotion). AUTHOR-ONLY fanout.
# Sessions:     6  (Mashed_pool0,1,2,3,5,6 pre-assigned — pool4 SKIPPED, leak-locked)
# Total RVAs:   137  (vehicle 80 + ai 57)
# Model:        Opus 4.8 (1M context)  (claude-opus-4-8[1m])
#               -> promote-c2 bulk-drain cap 60 RVAs/session (max bucket here: 27).
#               Same author-only cadence as batch_aj/ah/ai (Opus author-only
#               fanout + scripted ghidra-sweep). Per-RVA ~6k tokens; largest
#               bucket (27) ~ 162k + 30k startup, well clear of compaction.
#
# Pre-flight (verified at generation time, 2026-06-02):
#   * re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Queued section = EMPTY. batch_aj fully swept + re-classified
#     (sweep-20260602-1945; commits 3beccbb5 + 21e20ba1). SAFE to fan out.
#     (If a sweep is running when you start, STOP. Re-confirm Queued is empty.)
#   * 12 small application subsystems closed to C1=0 by batch_aj (input, track,
#     physics, smplfzx, powerups, camera, particle, sky, world-objects,
#     debug-overlay, video, hud). The next two CLOSEABLE pools are vehicle (80)
#     and ai (57). gameplay (792) and render (458) are deliberately NOT in this
#     batch — they are multi-batch campaigns of their own.
#   * Library-band screen over all 137 candidates:
#       - ai (57): ZERO hits. Clean application code.
#       - vehicle (80): 2 in the CRT band tail (004b3bf0, 004b3e40) AND a large
#         RenderWare-library cluster in the 0x004c/0x004e/0x0055/0x0057 high-
#         address tail (see Session 3). These do NOT auto-drop by range alone but
#         ARE vendored RW glue mislabeled `vehicle`. Session 3 carries explicit
#         reclass-OUT guidance. EXPECTED OUTCOME: vehicle pool closes as
#         "~N C2 vehicle + ~M reclass-OUT to third-party-library (kept-C1)",
#         NOT 80 -> all-C2.
#
# WHY this batch: continues the Phase-5 C1 drain. batch_aj closed the 12 small
# pools; vehicle + ai are the next two that fit in a single 6-session fanout and
# can be driven to C1=0 (vehicle with library reclass-outs). After ak, only the
# two big campaigns (gameplay 792, render 458) remain in the C1 pool.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AUTHOR-ONLY PROTOCOL (read before any session runs) — identical to batch_aj
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# These sessions are AUTHOR-ONLY. They do NOT promote anything themselves.
#   * DO write per-RVA .md plates into the session's bucket dir, using the
#     template heading `## Mechanical description` (NOT `## Purpose` — batch_aj
#     s3 used `## Purpose` and the sweep manifest builder could not resolve the
#     plates until they were renamed; do not repeat that).
#   * DO append ONE row to a PER-SESSION queue fragment:
#         re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s<N>.md   (NOT the shared re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md)
#   * DO NOT write hooks.csv. DO NOT run re-classify. DO NOT touch STUBS.md /
#     UNCERTAINTIES.md / DEFERRED.md directly. (Workers writing shared trackers
#     inline = races. See memory: feedback_concurrent_c1c2_with_frontend,
#     project_batch_ag_swept_reclassify_pending.)
#   * DO NOT mutate the master Ghidra project. No function_create, no rename, no
#     comment/bookmark writes to master. Read-only MCP only (function_at,
#     decomp_function, listing_*). function_create / renames are the SWEEP's job.
#   * DO NOT mint U-IDs or S-IDs. Mark new holes inline as bare `[UNCERTAIN]` in
#     the plate's `## Uncertainties` section with the gap described; the central
#     re-classify mints IDs from a reserved range. (Reserved this batch:
#     U-7500..U-7699, S-6200..S-6399 — central finalize owns the numbering.)
#   * Each session reads ONLY its pre-assigned pool clone (read-only). One commit
#     at end of session (plates + queue fragment), message:
#         "scribe(batch_ak s<N>): author <K> C1->C2 plates for <bucket>"
#
# FOLLOW-UP (single Opus session on main, AFTER all 6 commit — do NOT run now):
#   Scripted ghidra-sweep:
#     1. py -3.12 scripts/sweep_build_manifest.py --rows re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s1.md
#        ... re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s6.md --out sweep_manifest.json --date <D> --level C2
#        (confirm per-bucket plated == queued rva counts AND missing_md==0).
#     2. ghidra_eval apply via scripts/ghidra/sweep_apply_eval.py (one transaction:
#        plate comments + bookmarks; function_create only where a queue row flags
#        needs_function_create=).
#     3. Central re-classify on main: C1->C2 for every RVA whose plate is a clean
#        mechanical description; reclass-OUT the RW-library RVAs to
#        third-party-library[...] (KEPT C1, not promoted); mint U-/S- IDs centrally
#        from the reserved ranges.
#   Then merge the per-session queue fragments into re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SPECIAL FLAGS (carry into the relevant session block + the sweep)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   * RENDERWARE-LIBRARY RECLASS-OUT (Session 3, vehicle high-address tail): a
#     large fraction of the 0x004c/0x004e/0x0055/0x0057 RVAs are vendored
#     RenderWare (matrix/transform/clump/frame) or RenderWare-Physics-3.7 glue
#     mislabeled `vehicle` in hooks.csv. Several appear in batch_aj s4's RW stub
#     list (FUN_0055bab0, 00559c40, 0055b940, 0055c4a0, 004c0b30, 004c39b0,
#     004e69a0, 004e7e30, 0057c220/300/500). FOR EACH such RVA: if the decomp
#     shows a named RenderWare primitive shape (Rw*/Rp*/Rt*/rwID_* vocabulary,
#     matrix/clump/transform/physics-shape ops, provenance-string xref), DO NOT
#     plate as vehicle. Record subsystem_observed=third-party-library[renderware]
#     (or [RenderWare-Physics-3.7]) in the queue row and SKIP the C2 (vendored =>
#     library-tag drain, kept-C1, not hand-plate — see memory
#     project_qhull_rwphysics_island). Only plate genuine vehicle game code.
#   * CRT-BAND TAIL (Session 3): 004b3bf0 + 004b3e40 sit in the CRT band
#     (0x004a0000..0x004b3fff) but PAST the observed CRT block (~0x004b3a60).
#     004b3bf0 is referenced by track plates as a RW DFF/clump model loader, so
#     it is likely genuine RenderWare, NOT CRT. CONFIRM during decomp: if
#     FidDB-attested CRT (Library Function: tag, _name), reclass-OUT to
#     third-party-library[msvc-crt] keep-C1; if RW model loader, reclass-OUT to
#     third-party-library[renderware] keep-C1; do not plate as vehicle either way.
#   * 0x0057c220/300/500 sit just BELOW the qhull island start (0x0057c5b0).
#     Same caution as the qhull-adjacent physics RVAs in batch_aj s4.
#   * Subsystem labels below are HINTS, not claims. If decomp says otherwise,
#     record subsystem_observed=<X> in the queue row; the sweep reconciles.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POOL-SLOT HYGIENE (every session)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ak_s<N>"      # per-session marker, no race
#   Use the PRE-ASSIGNED Mashed_pool<S> clone directly (do NOT call
#   ghidra_pool.ps1 acquire — racing on acquire is forbidden). Mashed_pool4 is
#   SKIPPED this batch: it holds a leaked in-JVM project lock (see memory
#   feedback_mcp_leaked_project_lock — a failed MCP open poisons the slot for the
#   shared server's lifetime; the on-disk .lock survives deletion). If your
#   assigned slot is stale/leaked-locked, fall back to ANOTHER verified-free clone
#   (NOT acquire, NOT pool4), and record the ACTUAL slot used in the queue row.
#   ALWAYS program_close at end.
################################################################################


================================================================================
Session 1 — bucket_vehicle_00453f30_00482030  (promote-c2, 27-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool0 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 27)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_vehicle_00453f30_00482030/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s1.md  (per-session fragment; sweep merges later)

# Mission
First third of the vehicle C1 pool: the low-address game-code cluster
(0x0045-0x0048). Working hypothesis: vehicle (vehicle setup/update/physics-glue,
model + spawn). Read each end-to-end and write a mechanical C2 plate (full
control flow, every offset/constant cited, callees + callers noted). Confirm or
reclassify per RVA. This cluster is below the RW-library tail (Session 3), so
expect mostly genuine vehicle code — but still apply the library-confirm rule if
any RVA decodes as a RenderWare primitive.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA: read the path in that RVA's hooks.csv `file` column
  FIRST (deepen, don't restart). Drift-skip any already >= C2.
- hooks.csv rows: the 27 RVAs below (currently C1; do NOT edit hooks.csv).
- Cross-reference: Session 2 (rest of low/mid vehicle).

# Workflow
1. export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ak_s1"; use Mashed_pool0 read-only.
2. For each RVA: function_at -> decomp_function (-> listing_code_unit_at if no fn).
   Read the existing C1 plate and deepen to a full mechanical description.
3. Write re/analysis/bucket_vehicle_00453f30_00482030/<rva>.md with frontmatter
   (rva, name, size_bytes, confidence_target=C2, callees_depth1, callers_noted,
   opened_in_slot, session_date=2026-06-02) + "## Mechanical description" +
   "## Constants" table + "## Uncertainties" (bare [UNCERTAIN], do NOT mint) +
   "## Stubs encountered" (note, do NOT mint).
4. Append ONE row to re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s1.md (format = SESSION_RULES.md
   "Parallel-fanout scribe-queue pattern"): date, ak_s1, bucket, rvas=<csv>,
   confidence=C1->C2, note=<subsystem_observed + reclassifications + any
   needs_function_create=...>.
5. One commit: "scribe(batch_ak s1): author 27 C1->C2 plates for vehicle low cluster".

# Candidates (27)
00453f30,004548a0,004548e0,00456760,004587a0,00459290,0045ba10,0046b1c0,0046b540,00474d60,00476c10,00476cb0,004770c0,004781b0,0047a020,0047e9c0,0047ea60,00480b70,00480d60,00481750,00481780,00481810,004818d0,00481a30,00481d90,00481e00,00482030

# STOP-AND-ASK if
- function_at AND listing_code_unit_at both null for an RVA — surface + skip.
- An RVA is a library family (memcpy/_ftol2/RW primitive) — note in plate, do NOT
  rename; record subsystem_observed=third-party-library[...] in the queue row.


================================================================================
Session 2 — bucket_vehicle_004820e0_00485420  (promote-c2, 27-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool1 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 27)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_vehicle_004820e0_00485420/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s2.md

# Mission
Middle third of the vehicle C1 pool: the dense 0x0048xxxx cluster (vehicle
state/update core). Working hypothesis: vehicle. Confirm/reclassify per RVA.
Same library-confirm rule as Session 1.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA (hooks.csv `file` column). Drift-skip any >= C2.
- Cross-reference: Session 1 (low vehicle) + Session 3 (high/RW-library tail).

# Workflow
Identical to Session 1, but POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ak_s2", Mashed_pool1,
bucket dir bucket_vehicle_004820e0_00485420/, queue fragment
re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s2.md, commit "scribe(batch_ak s2): author 27 C1->C2 plates
for vehicle mid cluster".

# Candidates (27)
004820e0,00482140,004826d0,00482730,00482820,00482860,00482900,00482930,004829d0,00482ae0,00482c10,00483a30,00483a40,00483ca0,00483d10,00483ed0,00483fa0,00484000,00484060,004840b0,004840d0,004840f0,00485340,00485360,00485370,00485380,00485420

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 3 — bucket_vehicle_004922e0_0057c500  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain + RW reclass-OUT)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool2 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_vehicle_004922e0_0057c500/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s3.md

# Mission
High-address vehicle tail. *** THIS BUCKET IS RENDERWARE-LIBRARY-HEAVY. ***
Many of these RVAs are vendored RenderWare (matrix/transform/clump/frame) or
RenderWare-Physics-3.7 glue mislabeled `vehicle` because vehicle code calls them.
Your job is to SEPARATE genuine vehicle game code (plate C2) from RW library
(reclass-OUT, kept-C1, NOT plated). See the batch header's RENDERWARE-LIBRARY
RECLASS-OUT + CRT-BAND TAIL flags.

For EACH RVA:
- If decomp shows a named RenderWare primitive shape (Rw*/Rp*/Rt*/rwID_*
  vocabulary, matrix/clump/transform/physics-shape ops, provenance-string xref):
  record subsystem_observed=third-party-library[renderware] (or
  [RenderWare-Physics-3.7]) in the queue row, write a SHORT note-only plate
  (mechanical one-liner is fine), and FLAG it library-skip=<rva>. Do NOT author
  a full C2 vehicle plate; the sweep keeps it C1 under the library tag.
- Else (genuine vehicle game code): full C2 mechanical plate as in Session 1.

Known RW-suspects (from batch_aj s4 stub list / address proximity — CONFIRM each,
do not assume): 004b3bf0, 004b3e40 (CRT-band tail; 004b3bf0 = RW DFF loader),
004c0b30, 004c1040, 004c39b0 (RW matrix/transform), 004e69a0, 004e6ab0, 004e7e30
(RW clump/frame), 00559c40, 0055ae70, 0055b940, 0055bab0, 0055c4a0 (RW-Physics),
0057c220, 0057c300, 0057c500 (RW-Physics shape lifecycle, just below qhull
island 0x0057c5b0). 004b5190/5240/5320/5580 + 004b65a0/65b0 + 00546b10/00546e70
+ 004922e0/00496900 are UNCERTAIN — decode to decide.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA (hooks.csv `file` column). Drift-skip any >= C2.
- batch_aj s4 fragment (re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aj_s4.md) RW stub list for cross-ref.
- Cross-reference: Session 2 (mid vehicle).

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ak_s3", Mashed_pool2, bucket dir
bucket_vehicle_004922e0_0057c500/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s3.md.
The queue row MUST list, separately: rvas_c2=<genuine vehicle> and
library_skip=<reclass-OUT csv with the third-party-library[...] tag per RVA>.
Commit "scribe(batch_ak s3): author vehicle-tail plates + RW library-skips".

# Candidates (26)
004922e0,00496900,004b3bf0,004b3e40,004b5190,004b5240,004b5320,004b5580,004b65a0,004b65b0,004c0b30,004c1040,004c39b0,004e69a0,004e6ab0,004e7e30,00546b10,00546e70,00559c40,0055ae70,0055b940,0055bab0,0055c4a0,0057c220,0057c300,0057c500

# STOP-AND-ASK if
- An RVA is ambiguous vehicle-vs-RW after decomp — write the plate with the
  mechanical description + a bare [UNCERTAIN] noting the boundary, record
  subsystem_observed with your best call, and surface it in the queue row note.
  Do NOT block the rest of the bucket.
- 0x0057c220/300/500 show qhull shape (hull/facet/vertex vocab): tag
  third-party-library[qhull-2002.1] and library-skip (see project_qhull_rwphysics_island).


================================================================================
Session 4 — bucket_ai_00407a40_00415880  (promote-c2, 19-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool3 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 19)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_ai_00407a40_00415880/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s4.md

# Mission
First third of the ai C1 pool: the dense 0x0041xxxx cluster (AI decision/update
core). Working hypothesis: ai (CPU-racer behavior, path/target logic). Clean
application code (no library-band hits). Confirm/reclassify per RVA.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA (hooks.csv `file` column). Drift-skip any >= C2.
- Cross-reference: Sessions 5-6 (rest of ai).

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ak_s4", Mashed_pool3, bucket dir
bucket_ai_00407a40_00415880/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s4.md, commit
"scribe(batch_ak s4): author 19 C1->C2 plates for ai low cluster".

# Candidates (19)
00407a40,00408af0,0040e4a0,00413fe0,00414030,00414300,00414490,00414570,004148b0,00414a70,00414c30,00414f00,00415020,004150e0,00415190,00415200,00415220,00415860,00415880

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 5 — bucket_ai_00415d00_00452ea0  (promote-c2, 19-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool5 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire; pool4 SKIPPED)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 19)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_ai_00415d00_00452ea0/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s5.md

# Mission
Middle third of the ai C1 pool (0x0041-0x0044 spread). Working hypothesis: ai.
Confirm/reclassify per RVA.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA (hooks.csv `file` column). Drift-skip any >= C2.
- Cross-reference: Sessions 4 + 6.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ak_s5", Mashed_pool5, bucket dir
bucket_ai_00415d00_00452ea0/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s5.md, commit
"scribe(batch_ak s5): author 19 C1->C2 plates for ai mid cluster".

# Candidates (19)
00415d00,00415e20,00416060,004161e0,00416250,00416a30,00422ba0,004252c0,00426c90,00429840,00429860,00442cc0,00443080,00443300,00443440,00443d10,00443dc0,00452160,00452ea0

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 6 — bucket_ai_00452eb0_004c3df0  (promote-c2, 19-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool6 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 19)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_ai_00452eb0_004c3df0/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s6.md

# Mission
High third of the ai C1 pool (0x0045-0x004c spread; widest cluster). Working
hypothesis: ai. Confirm/reclassify per RVA. A few high-address RVAs (0x004715a0,
0x004c3df0) may be RW/math helpers called by AI — apply the library-confirm rule
if so.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA (hooks.csv `file` column). Drift-skip any >= C2.
- Cross-reference: Sessions 4 + 5.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ak_s6", Mashed_pool6, bucket dir
bucket_ai_00452eb0_004c3df0/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ak_s6.md, commit
"scribe(batch_ak s6): author 19 C1->C2 plates for ai high cluster".

# Candidates (19)
00452eb0,00455b40,0045a0f0,0046c730,0046c750,0046cc10,0046d4a0,0046d510,0046d570,0046d6a0,0046d6d0,0046d780,0046d7f0,004715a0,004783f0,00478660,00484c70,0048a630,004c3df0

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1, plus library-confirm for 004715a0 / 004c3df0.


################################################################################
# END batch_ak.txt — 137 RVAs across 6 author-only sessions.
# After all 6 commit: run the scripted ghidra-sweep on main (see header), then
# central re-classify C1->C2 + reclass-OUT the RW-library RVAs (kept-C1) + merge
# per-session queue fragments into re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained.
# Expected outcome: ai C1 -> 0 (all 57 genuine application code); vehicle C1 -> 0
# split as (~N C2 vehicle + ~M reclass-OUT to third-party-library, kept-C1).
# After ak, the only remaining C1 application pools are the two big multi-batch
# campaigns: gameplay (792) and render (458).
################################################################################
